---
title: "Can You Validate Me?"
subtitle: "Unit 5 Stretch Task — Star Wars Survey Chart Replication"
author: "Vincent Gentry"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute:
  warning: false

---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import polars.selectors as cs
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)

data_url = "https://byuidatascience.github.io/DS250-Course-Polars/Data/StarWars_with_emails.csv"
```

## Data Loading & Cleaning

The data cleaning mirrors the approach from Task 3 — the two-row header is merged
into a single set of column names before the data rows are read in.

```{python}
a = pl.Series(
    pl.read_csv(data_url, skip_rows=10, n_rows=1, has_header=False,
                encoding="utf8-lossy").row(0)
)

a[2] = "seen_any"

a = (
    a.str.replace(r".+seen.+",       "seen_")
     .str.replace(r".+rank.+",       "rank_")
     .str.replace(r"(.+favorably.+)", "fav_")
     .fill_null(strategy="forward")
     .str.replace("\uFFFD+", "")
     .str.strip_chars()
     .str.to_lowercase()
     .str.replace_all(" ", "_")
     .str.replace(r"location(.+)", "location")
)

a[3]  = "star_wars_fan"
a[30] = "who_shot_first"
a[31] = "know_expanded_universe"
a[32] = "expanded_universe_fan"
a[33] = "star_trek_fan"

b = pl.Series(
    pl.read_csv(data_url, skip_rows=11, n_rows=1, has_header=False,
                encoding="utf8-lossy").row(0)
)

b = (
    b.str.replace(r"Star Wars: (Episode \w+) .+", "${1}")
     .str.replace("Response", "")
     .str.replace_all(" ", "_")
     .fill_null("")
     .str.strip_chars()
)

new_names = (a + b).to_list()

df = pl.read_csv(
    data_url, skip_rows=12, has_header=False,
    new_columns=new_names
)

print(f"Rows: {len(df)}  |  Columns: {len(df.columns)}")
```

---

## Chart 1 — "Which 'Star Wars' Films Have You Seen?"


```{python}
fans = df.filter(pl.col("seen_any") == "Yes")
n_fans = len(fans)

seen_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("seen_") and c != "seen_any"]

episode_labels = {
    "seen_Episode_I":   "The Phantom Menace",
    "seen_Episode_II":  "Attack of the Clones",
    "seen_Episode_III": "Revenge of the Sith",
    "seen_Episode_IV":  "A New Hope",
    "seen_Episode_V":   "The Empire Strikes Back",
    "seen_Episode_VI":  "Return of the Jedi",
}

seen_counts = pl.DataFrame({
    "col":   seen_cols,
    "n_seen": [fans[c].drop_nulls().len() for c in seen_cols],
}).with_columns(
    (pl.col("n_seen") / n_fans * 100).alias("pct"),
    pl.col("col").replace(episode_labels).alias("film"),
).sort("pct", descending=True).with_columns(
    (pl.col("pct").round(0).cast(pl.Int32).cast(pl.String) + "%").alias("label")
)

print(seen_counts.select(["film", "pct"]))
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(seen_counts, aes(x="pct", y=as_discrete("film", order_by="pct")))
    + geom_bar(
        stat="identity",
        fill="#008fd5",
        width=0.6,
    )
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="label"),
        hjust=-0.1,
        size=9,
        color="#555555",
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(
        limits=[0, 95],
        breaks=[0, 20, 40, 60, 80],
        labels=["0%", "20%", "40%", "60%", "80%"],
    )
    + labs(
        title="Which 'Star Wars' Films Have You Seen?",
        subtitle=f"Of {n_fans:,} respondents who have seen any film",
        x="",
        y="",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=14, face="bold"),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=10, color="#555555"),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_line(color="#e0e0e0"),
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks=element_blank(),
        axis_text=element_text(size=10),
    )
)
```

---

## Chart 2 — "What's the Best 'Star Wars' Movie?

```{python}
seen_all = fans.filter(
    pl.all_horizontal(
        [pl.col(c).is_not_null() for c in seen_cols]
    )
)
n_all = len(seen_all)

rank_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("rank_")]

episode_rank_labels = {
    "rank_Episode_I":   "The Phantom Menace",
    "rank_Episode_II":  "Attack of the Clones",
    "rank_Episode_III": "Revenge of the Sith",
    "rank_Episode_IV":  "A New Hope",
    "rank_Episode_V":   "The Empire Strikes Back",
    "rank_Episode_VI":  "Return of the Jedi",
}

ranked_best = pl.DataFrame({
    "col": rank_cols,
    "n_best": [
        seen_all.filter(
            pl.col(c).cast(pl.Int32, strict=False) == 1
        ).shape[0]
        for c in rank_cols
    ],
}).with_columns(
    (pl.col("n_best") / n_all * 100).alias("pct_best"),
    pl.col("col").replace(episode_rank_labels).alias("film"),
).sort("pct_best", descending=True).with_columns(
    (pl.col("pct_best").round(0).cast(pl.Int32).cast(pl.String) + "%").alias("label")
)

print(ranked_best.select(["film", "pct_best"]))
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(ranked_best, aes(x="pct_best", y=as_discrete("film", order_by="pct_best")))
    + geom_bar(
        stat="identity",
        fill="#008fd5",
        width=0.6,
    )
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="label"),
        hjust=-0.1,
        size=9,
        color="#555555",
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(
        limits=[0, 55],
        breaks=[0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50],
        labels=["0%", "10%", "20%", "30%", "40%", "50%"],
    )
    + labs(
        title="What's the Best 'Star Wars' Movie?",
        subtitle=f"Of {n_all:,} respondents who have seen all 6 films, % who ranked each film #1",
        x="",
        y="",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=14, face="bold"),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=10, color="#555555"),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_line(color="#e0e0e0"),
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks=element_blank(),
        axis_text=element_text(size=10),
    )
)
```


---

## Validation

### Chart 1 — Films Seen

Pretty much what you'd expect, the original trilogy (Episodes IV–VI) was seen by around 79–83% of fans, and the prequels drop off to somewhere in the 53–60% range. Our chart looks basically the same as the one in the article, so the data checks out here.

### Chart 2 — Best Film

*The Empire Strikes Back* is the most popular, grabbing about 36% of #1 votes, with *A New Hope* not too far behind at around 25%. The prequels are basically a rounding error. That matches the article pretty closely, so the ranking data looks good too.

### Overall

Both charts line up well enough with the article that we can trust the dataset. The numbers aren't going to be pixel-perfect since these are survey estimates, but the story they tell is the same.
